---
title: "Country Analysis"
---

## Introduction

This page explores how life expectancy, GDP per capita, and population change over time for a single country in the Gapminder dataset.

```{python}
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# Load dataset
df = pd.read_csv("gapminder.csv")

# Check column names
df.columns

``` 

```{python}
# choosing US as country
country_name = "United States"

country_df = df[df["country"] == country_name].sort_values("year")

country_df.head()

plt.figure(figsize=(10, 8))

# Life expectancy
plt.subplot(3, 1, 1)
plt.plot(country_df["year"], country_df["life_expectancy"])
plt.title(f"{country_name}: Trends Over Time")
plt.ylabel("Life Expectancy")

# GDP per capita
plt.subplot(3, 1, 2)
plt.plot(country_df["year"], country_df["gdp_per_capita"])
plt.ylabel("GDP per Capita")

# Population
plt.subplot(3, 1, 3)
plt.plot(country_df["year"], country_df["population_millions"])
plt.xlabel("Year")
plt.ylabel("Population (millions)")

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

``` 

## Interpretation

For the United States, life expectancy generally increases over time, GDP per capita significantly rises, and population steadily increases as well. This indicates economic and health improvements over time.
